title: Spectral Decomposition Studio
description: A rigged figure stands on a carbon fibre platform surrounded by silk poufs acting as spectral energy nodes. The user manipulates post-processing effect chains—bloom, chromatic aberration, film grain, vignette, and color grading—by dragging energy between the figure and pouf nodes, with a conservation law ensuring total effect intensity remains constant across all channels. Undo/redo lets the user backtrack through complex multi-node configurations.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load RiggedFigure.glb centered at origin on a ground plane textured with CarbonFibre.glb's material (extract its PBR textures and apply to a 6x6 plane). Place 5 SpecularSilkPouf.glb instances in a pentagon formation around the figure at radius 3, each tinted a unique color (red, green, blue, magenta, cyan) representing: Bloom, ChromaticAberration, FilmGrain, Vignette, and ColorBalance. Camera: perspective at (0,3,7) looking at origin with OrbitControls. Lighting: one directional light plus ambient, plus a point light per pouf matching its color. Post-processing pipeline: UnrealBloomPass, ShaderPass for chromatic aberration, film grain, vignette, and color balance (write simple custom shaders). CONSERVATION LAW: total effect intensity across all 5 channels always equals exactly 5.0 (each starts at 1.0). INTERACTIONS: (1) Click a pouf to select it (outline highlight). (2) Click a second pouf to create a transfer link. (3) Drag a slider that appears to transfer intensity from first to second pouf (both update, sum preserved). (4) Click the figure to enter Distribution Mode where dragging vertically redistributes intensity from the figure's glow (bloom) equally to all other channels. (5) Right-click a pouf to lock its value (locked poufs cannot be transfer targets). (6) Double-click the figure to reset all to 1.0. (7) Press Ctrl+Z for undo and Ctrl+Y for redo, maintaining a history stack of up to 30 states. Each pouf scales (0.5-1.5x) proportional to its intensity. The figure's skeleton should have a subtle idle animation mixed from the GLB. Display a HUD bar chart showing all 5 channel intensities with numeric labels and the conservation total. Render at window size with antialiasing and tone mapping set to ACESFilmic.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomIntensity: number,         // current bloom effect strength (0.0-5.0)
  chromaticIntensity: number,         // current chromatic aberration strength (0.0-5.0)
  filmGrainIntensity: number,         // current film grain strength (0.0-5.0)
  vignetteIntensity: number,         // current vignette effect strength (0.0-5.0)
  colorBalanceIntensity: number,         // current color balance strength (0.0-5.0)
  totalIntensity: number,         // sum of all effect intensities (must equal 5.0)
  selectedPoufIndex: number,         // index of first selected pouf (-1 if none, 0-4 for poufs)
  transferTargetIndex: number,         // index of second selected pouf for transfer (-1 if none)
  isDistributionMode: boolean,         // true when figure is clicked for vertical drag redistribution
  transferAmount: number,         // current amount being transferred between poufs (0.0-5.0)
  activeLinkCount: number,         // number of active transfer operations completed
  figureAnimationPhase: number,         // animation phase of rigged figure (0.0-1.0)
}
